en.extract:
The Papuan white-eye (Zosterops novaeguineae), sometimes known as the New Guinea white-eye, is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It is found in the Aru Islands and New Guinea (mainly Huon, Bird's Head and Papuan Peninsula). Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. The species was first classified in 1878, and has a conservation status of Least Concern.

The '''Papuan white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops novaeguineae'''''), sometimes known as the '''New Guinea white-eye''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is found in the [[Aru Islands]] and [[New Guinea]] (mainly [[Huon Peninsula|Huon]], [[Bird's Head Peninsula|Bird's Head]] and [[Papuan Peninsula]]). Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. The species was first classified in 1878, and has a [[conservation status]] of [[Least-concern species|Least Concern]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Zosterops novaeguineae Salvadori, 1878|url=https://www.gbif.org/species/2489409|access-date=2021-04-09|website=www.gbif.org|language=en}}</ref>
